name: Chef Galati's Spezzatino Di Vitello (Veal Ste
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Veal shoulder, boneless, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 can Carrots, peeled and cubed (14 to 15 oz. can)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon in a large saucepot until crisp. Remove the bacon and set aside, leaving 2 tablespoons of drippings in the pot.
2. Heat the drippings until hot. Add veal pieces, cooking them on all sides until browned. Remove the veal and set aside.
3. Add shallots and garlic to the pot and sauté for 2 minutes.
4. Stir in flour and cook for another minute.
5. Return the veal to the pot. Stir in stock, salt, pepper, 1-1/2 cups of wine, and Worcestershire sauce. Bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, until the veal is tender (about 1-1/2 hours).
7. In a skillet, melt butter. Add mushrooms and sauté for 3 minutes. Keep warm.
8. Add carrots, onions, mushrooms, bacon, and remaining wine to the stew during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
9. Transfer the stew to a serving casserole dish. Sprinkle with chopped parsley, if desired.
SOURCE: A Lea & Perrins Worcestershire Sauce advertisement in Southern Living Magazine, sometime in 1979. Typed for you by Nancy Coleman.
